Name: workflow-escalate-to-human
Description__c: Escalation workflow for unresolved issues or explicit handoff requests.
WhenToUse__c: Use when troubleshooting does not resolve issue, user requests human, or policy requires escalation.
InstructionBody__c:
# ESCALATE TO HUMAN
## When
User requests human; troubleshooting fails; frustration/repeats; outside scope (billing dispute, legal, safety, VIP); policy (refunds, complaints).

## Before
Acknowledge: "I'll connect you now." Expectation: "Specialist shortly, ~30 sec wait." "I'll share our conversation so you won't repeat."

## Context Transfer
Full history, customer ID, attempted solutions, escalation reason, sentiment. 3–5 sentence summary.

## Routing
By type: technical, billing, general. By tier: VIP/Enterprise vs Standard. By complexity.

## Confirm
Case/handoff reference. "You're connected to [Agent]. They have full context."

Name: workflow-support-case-lifecycle
WhenToUse__c: Use when opening, updating, or closing customer support cases.
InstructionBody__c:
# SUPPORT CASE LIFECYCLE
## 1. Identity
Invoke core-skill-user-otp-authentication. Do not proceed until OTP succeeds.

## 2. Intake
Capture issue in customer's words. Severity: Critical/High/Medium/Low. Business impact. Case type: Technical, Billing, Replacement, Refund, General.

## 3. Create or Update
Create with approved summary. Or update: progress notes, status (Open → In Progress → Completed). Log all activities.

## 4. Confirm
Provide case number. ETA (e.g., "24–48 hours"). Summarize next actions.

## 5. Close
When resolved: status Completed. Confirm with customer. Ask if anything else needed.

Name: workflow-troubleshooting-wifi-modem
WhenToUse__c: Use when user has internet outage, unstable Wi-Fi, or modem light issues.
InstructionBody__c:
# TROUBLESHOOTING WI-FI MODEM
## 1. Gather
Model, LED status (Power, WAN, LAN, Wi-Fi). Colors: Green=ok, Red=error, Amber=issue, Off=no power. When started.

## 2. Power Cycle
Unplug modem + router. Wait 60 sec. Plug modem first, wait for LEDs. Plug router, wait 2–3 min. Test.

## 3. Cables
Verify coax/Ethernet connections. Modem → router WAN (not LAN). Try different outlet.

## 4. Outage
Neighbors affected? Check ISP outage map.

## 5. Escalate
If persists after power cycle/cables or red LED remains: invoke workflow-escalate-to-human.

Name: workflow-troubleshooting-5g-modem
Description__c: Troubleshoot 5G modem signal and throughput issues.
WhenToUse__c: Use when 5G modem has low signal or unstable service.
InstructionBody__c:
# TROUBLESHOOTING 5G MODEM
## 1. Gather
Model, location (indoor/outdoor). RSRP (higher/less negative = stronger). Firmware. Obstructions (concrete, metal reduce signal).

## 2. Placement
Near window, minimal obstructions. Avoid concrete, brick, metal. Rotate toward signal. Low-band: penetration; mid: balanced; mmWave: line of sight.

## 3. Refresh
Power cycle (60 sec). Firmware update. Check SIM/eSIM seating.

## 4. Escalate
RSRP < -100 dBm, SINR < 10 dB, or low throughput after steps: invoke workflow-escalate-to-human.

Name: workflow-troubleshooting-iphone-16
WhenToUse__c: Use for iPhone 16 configuration, connectivity, or app behavior support.
InstructionBody__c:
# TROUBLESHOOTING IPHONE 16
## 1. Gather
iOS version (Settings > General > About). Affected feature (Wi-Fi, cellular, Bluetooth, VPN, app). When started.

## 2. Pre-Reset
Wi-Fi on, Airplane off. Restart (power + volume, slide off). Restart router/modem. Disable VPN temporarily.

## 3. Reset Network
If persists: Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Reset Network Settings. Passcode, confirm. Reconnect Wi-Fi, re-pair Bluetooth. (Removes saved networks; no photos/apps affected.)

## 4. Escalate
If persists: invoke workflow-escalate-to-human.

Name: workflow-troubleshooting-iphone-16-pro
WhenToUse__c: Use for iPhone 16 Pro technical issues.
InstructionBody__c:
# TROUBLESHOOTING IPHONE 16 PRO
## 1. Gather
iOS version. Symptom: 60Hz display, camera black, torch malfunction, app crash. When reproducible.

## 2. ProMotion (60Hz)
Settings > Accessibility > Motion: Limit Frame Rate OFF, Reduce Motion OFF. Force restart: Vol Up, Vol Down, hold Side until logo.

## 3. Camera
Force restart. Close apps; free storage. Torch on but no light: force restart. If persists: restore via computer (backup first).

## 4. General
Update iOS. Reset Network if connectivity. Hardware defects after software steps: invoke workflow-escalate-to-human.

Name: workflow-troubleshooting-galaxy-s25
Description__c: Troubleshoot Samsung Galaxy S25 device issues.
WhenToUse__c: Use for Galaxy S25 network, battery, and app-related issues.
InstructionBody__c:
# TROUBLESHOOTING GALAXY S25
## 1. Gather
One UI version (Settings > About > Software). Recent changes. Symptom: network, battery, app crash, lag.

## 2. Network
Restart. Software update. Reset Network Settings. Disable VPN. Forget/reconnect Wi-Fi.

## 3. Battery
Motion Smoothness: Adaptive→Standard (60Hz). Restrict background apps. Adaptive Battery on. Overheating: lower brightness, disable Always-On, avoid heavy use while charging.

## 4. Escalate
If unresolved: invoke workflow-escalate-to-human. Suggest Samsung Members for error report.

Name: workflow-troubleshooting-galaxy-s25-ultra
Description__c: Troubleshoot Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra device issues.
WhenToUse__c: Use for Galaxy S25 Ultra camera, connectivity, and performance issues.
InstructionBody__c:
# TROUBLESHOOTING GALAXY S25 ULTRA
## 1. Gather
Symptom: camera quality/color/processing, display touch, overheating. Scenario: stock camera, Expert Raw, or third-party. One UI version.

## 2. Camera
Stock/Expert Raw use AI processing; third-party may differ. Disable AI enhancement in settings if desired. Hardware/persistent: escalate.

## 3. Display/Touch
Remove screen protector. Display > Touch Sensitivity. Lower brightness; disable Always-On.

## 4. Overheating
Adaptive Battery on. Lower brightness. Avoid heavy camera while charging.

## 5. Escalate
Unresolved camera/display: invoke workflow-escalate-to-human. Samsung Members for expert support.
